briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. We will now begin the climate briefing for March 24, 2026.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Acceleration of Carbon Neutrality and Green Transition Efforts
sections.content: Today, diverse efforts by local governments, businesses, and civil society to achieve carbon neutrality goals were highlighted domestically. Daegu City is accelerating its carbon neutrality implementation with a target of reducing greenhouse gas emissions by 45% by 2030 [7, 8], and Cheongju City is also accelerating its progress towards carbon neutrality by raising this year's greenhouse gas reduction target to 24.5% [23, 27]. Gumi National Industrial Complex is also preparing for a leap forward, embracing carbon neutrality as an opportunity for new growth engines [9, 12]. Chungnam Province, while promoting agricultural and fishing innovation and the carbon neutrality agenda within local communities, urged the establishment of a fair compensation system for the contributions of rural and fishing villages [13, 18, 24, 26]. Additionally, active actions by various stakeholders continue, including KT&G being selected as an excellent company in the climate change and water resource management sectors [3], discussions underway regarding the introduction of an automatic payment system for electric vehicle charging [2], Incheon City's district unit plan reflecting carbon neutrality values [21], and carbon neutrality tree-planting campaigns held across the country [10, 11, 17, 22, 25, 30].

sections.title: ⚠️ The Dual Nature of Renewable Energy Transition: Focusing on Domestic Cases
sections.content: While the expansion of renewable energy continues, discussions regarding safety issues and the distribution of generation profits have also been raised. Efforts to expand new and renewable energy infrastructure are ongoing, with the Taean offshore wind power complex being conditionally designated [4], and the government announced plans to expand renewable energy by 7GW this year [35]. However, a tragic accident occurred in Yeongdeok, where a wind power-related fire resulted in three fatalities [5], and serious concerns are being raised about the safety of renewable energy and the management of aging facilities, with Yeongdeok County pursuing complete demolition due to a series of accidents involving old wind turbines [31, 41, 42]. Alongside these issues, challenges remain for a stable and equitable renewable energy transition, including exploring the use of green hydrogen to address the intermittency of renewable energy [33] and arguments that generation profits should be shared with citizens [34].

sections.title: 🔴 Worsening Global Climate Crisis and Spreading Impacts
sections.content: Globally, the threats posed by climate change are accelerating, and their impacts are spreading widely. Across the United States, record-breaking March heatwaves continue to set new records [49], and research suggests that climate change could lead to billions of additional days of anxiety and depression [53]. Furthermore, warnings regarding ecosystems and public health continue, including a sharp decline in pastures [56], the spread of dangerous Aspergillus fungi [57], and the potential increase in antibiotic-resistant superbugs due to drought [58]. While the overall vulnerability of ecosystems is being revealed, with the large heath butterfly being dubbed 'the canary in the coal mine of climate change' [60], phenomena such as winters starting earlier but snow melting faster [66], and rising home insurance premiums [67] and disruptions in major agricultural production [69] due to climate change clearly demonstrate the severity of the climate crisis humanity faces.

sections.title: 🌍 International Community's Climate Actions and Policy Trends
sections.content: Meanwhile, the international community recognizes the severity of climate change and is exploring policy changes alongside various response efforts. India officially launched the 'ICM Portal,' a one-stop platform for its carbon credit market, strengthening market-based climate action [28], and the accelerated solar transition by major Indian corporations and LG Electronics' acquisition of a stake in an Indian solar power plant indicate an expansion of renewable energy investment in the Asian region [36, 39]. New York State is focusing on fostering climate awareness among future generations by mandating climate change education [48], and Ofcom, the UK's communications regulator, has begun investigating complaints related to climate change denial to ensure information accuracy [45, 76]. The argument that ecological boundaries should be integrated into the climate regime after COP30 [62] and the Scottish Government's announcement of its final climate change plan [73] demonstrate the increasing importance of climate action across countries and industries worldwide.

briefing.closing: Today's briefing has highlighted the severe impacts of climate change, alongside the convergence of diverse domestic and international efforts to overcome them. It is imperative that we gather our collective wisdom to address the climate crisis through continuous attention and cooperation. This concludes the climate briefing for March 24, 2026.

articles.original_title: Ofcom to investigate complaints of climate change denial for first time since 2017
articles.description: Exclusive: UK regulator makes U-turn over TalkTV and TalkRadio complaints after claims it let some broadcasters ‘spout dangerous climate lies’A U-turn by the UK’s broadcasting regulator Ofcom means it will investigate complaints of climate change denial on television and radio for the first time since 2017. The move marks a victory for campaigners who have accused the regulator of allowing some broadcasters “to spout dangerous climate lies” and “flout” rules on accuracy and impartiality.Complain

summary.phenomenon: Ofcom, the UK's broadcasting regulator, will investigate complaints related to climate change denial on television and radio for the first time since 2017.
summary.cause: This follows accusations from campaign groups that some broadcasters have 'spouted dangerous climate lies' and 'flouted' rules on accuracy and impartiality.
